summary: Top quantitative and qualitative findings from running the Autonomous AI Scientist pipeline across 3 academic papers (32 gaps, 96 pairwise comparisons).

quantitative_findings.finding: System beats keyword-only baseline in 65.1% of pairwise comparisons
quantitative_findings.interpretation: Across all 96 judge decisions, the pipeline's hypothesis—after critic scoring, debate, and reflection—was preferred over the naive baseline in nearly two-thirds of cases.

quantitative_findings.finding: Average hypothesis quality score is 7.11 / 10, vs. 5.72 for baseline (+24.4%)
quantitative_findings.interpretation: Critic scoring + debate refinement consistently elevate raw LLM output to near-publication-quality research directions.

quantitative_findings.finding: 69.1% of system hypotheses rated KEEP by the LLM critic
quantitative_findings.interpretation: Roughly 7 in 10 generated hypotheses clear the KEEP threshold (total score ≥ 12), versus 43.8% for the baseline. The debate and critic loop filters weak ideas before they reach the final report.

quantitative_findings.finding: AgentA and AgentB agree 79.5% of the time on which hypothesis is better
quantitative_findings.interpretation: High inter-agent agreement suggests the LLM critic rubric (clarity / novelty / feasibility) is stable and reproducible across identical prompts. paper_2 shows the lowest agreement (67%), indicating harder or more ambiguous gaps.

quantitative_findings.finding: paper_3 achieves the highest win rate (71%) and agreement (88%), paper_2 the lowest (54%, 67%)
quantitative_findings.interpretation: Performance variance across papers (win_rate range: 0.54 – 0.71) suggests gap quality and domain familiarity influence system performance. Papers where gap statements are more concrete yield better hypotheses.

qualitative_findings.finding: Multi-agent debate consistently surfaces the higher-quality hypothesis
qualitative_findings.evidence: In paper_1, G12 (robustness) is the only gap where AgentB outscores AgentA (13 vs 12). The debate mechanism correctly selected AgentB's ablation-based approach over AgentA's repair-heuristic proposal.

qualitative_findings.finding: Critic scoring catches under-specified hypotheses before they enter the final report
qualitative_findings.evidence: G23 (problem_formulation) received a REVISE verdict (total=10) because the hypothesis lacked an explicit comparison baseline. G31 (future_work) was REJECTED due to feasibility=2 — the meta-controller concept was too abstract.

qualitative_findings.finding: Scalability and data_quality gaps yield the strongest hypotheses
qualitative_findings.evidence: All three scalability gaps (G5, G13, G16) and data_quality gaps (G1, G9, G27) in paper_1 received the maximum sum score of 14 (5+5+4), suggesting the pipeline excels at identifying and refining computational performance improvements.

limitations_observed: All three evaluation_report.json files are mocked — real LLM judge calls have not yet been executed.
Cross-paper variance may partly reflect differences in PDF text quality rather than true domain difficulty.
RAG retrieval influence on hypothesis quality is unmeasured in current schema.
